beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, chat with dog about the day ahead
beats.reasoning: Marcus wakes at home with full energy on a sunny Monday; morning routine folds into a single beat. Early start suits his mood and the nice weather.

beats.activity: Drive to Capitol Hill office, listening to real estate market podcast
beats.reasoning: Commute from Wallingford to Capitol Hill across town; 35 min budgeted for Monday morning traffic and Light Rail closure (avoids transit anyway).

beats.activity: Morning client meetings and condo listing prep at office
beats.reasoning: Marcus works through the morning on client meetings and listings; wraps up before 11am FIFA match kickoff, which he wants to catch with colleagues or at a nearby bar.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match with coworkers at nearby bar
beats.reasoning: USA vs Wales match airs at 11am PDT; Marcus attends exactly on time at a Capitol Hill bar with coworkers—no crowds needed, just a few colleagues. Stays for full first half and early second half.

beats.activity: Lunch and debrief with coworkers at bar
beats.reasoning: Marcus stays at the bar for a casual lunch and post-match conversation; keeps it low-key with close work friends rather than heading elsewhere.

beats.activity: Walk back to office, picking up a few mushroom forage spots in nearby green areas
beats.reasoning: Beautiful sunny day; Marcus takes a scenic walk back to the office via some Capitol Hill parks, scouting mushroom spots for future foraging—merges commute and hobby on a gorgeous afternoon.

beats.activity: Afternoon client showings and property paperwork
beats.reasoning: Back to work for afternoon client showings and condo listing follow-ups; solid afternoon block before heading home.

beats.activity: Drive home to Wallingford, decompressing from work day
beats.reasoning: 40 min commute home from Capitol Hill to Wallingford; Light Rail closure no longer in effect but Marcus prefers driving anyway. Unwinds on the drive.

beats.activity: Make dinner (fresh salad, grilled fish) and eat with dog nearby
beats.reasoning: Marcus prepares a healthy dinner at home, chatting with his dog as if they're dinner companions; suits his patient, grounded personality and ends the day low-key.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: read real estate market reports, listen to music, rest on couch with dog
beats.reasoning: Marcus winds down at home with light reading and music, unwinding before bed; no big social plans—he values quiet time with close connections (his dog counts).

diary: Woke up early and had my usual chat with the dog—he seemed optimistic about the day, which helped. The drive to Capitol Hill was smooth, and I knocked out those morning client meetings before things got interesting. Around 11, half the office migrated to Kremwerk to catch the World Cup match. USA vs Wales had everyone wound up; there's something about soccer that brings out the competitive energy in people who don't normally care about sports. We stayed through lunch, debriefing about the game and work in equal measure. On my walk back, I found some decent mushroom spots in the green pockets near the office—nothing fancy, but enough to feel connected to something real. The afternoon was heavy with showings and paperwork, but the day had already peaked, you know? Drive home felt restorative. Made a simple dinner with fresh fish and sat with the dog while the evening wound down. Ended up reading market reports on the couch, which sounds boring but felt exactly right.
thought_process: The World Cup match was a welcome break—I almost skipped it, thinking I should power through work, but watching everyone gather around the screen reminded me that balance matters. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive, but I noticed the energy shift downtown with bars opening early. My mood lifted during that lunch hour, honestly. By afternoon, I was running on that residual good feeling, which probably made me sharper with clients. The mushroom foraging detour wasn't planned; I just needed a few minutes outside after the intensity of the bar. It grounded me. By evening, I was genuinely tired but content—the kind of tired that comes from a full, varied day rather than burnout.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
